LAW AND MOTION 301, DEFENDANT RICHARD GLICKMAN'S DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT. AFTER THE ARGUMENT, THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING WITH MODIFICATIONS AS FOLLOWS: DEFENDANT RICHARD GLICKMAN'S DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS FURTHER SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE WITHIN 15 DAYS TO AMEND TO THE FIRST, SECOND, THIRD AND FIFTH CAUSES OF ACTION TO IDENTIFY AGAINST WHOM THE CLAIMS ARE ASSERTED AND WHETHER THEY REFER TO DEFENDANT GLICKMAN. ORDER(WITH ATTACHMENT) WAS SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: JOHN K. STEWART, REPORTER: PATRICK HEARD, CSR # 11055

LAW AND MOTION 301, DEFENDANTS WILLIAM LUKENS, PHILLIP BASS, JENNIFER JONAK, DANIEL O'CONNELL'S DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT. AFTER THE ARGUMENT, THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING WITH MODIFICATIONS AS FOLLOWS: DEFENDANTS WILLIAM LUKENS, PHILLIP BASS, JENNIFER JONAK, DANIEL O'CONNELL'S DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT JOINED IN BY DEFENDANT GLICKMAN ARE SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND THE FIRST(INCLUDING ISSUE RE STANDING, EG. TRUSTEE?) SECOND & FOURTH CAUSES OF ACTION WITH RESPECT TO THE STATUTE OF LIMITATIONS, TO THE THIRD CAUSE OF ACTION TO IDENTIFY TO WHOM THE CLAIM IS ASSERTED AGAINST, AND TO THE FIFTH CAUSE OF ACTION WITH RESPECT TO THE ELEMENTS OF FRAUD AND TO WHOM THE CLAIM IS ASSERTED AGAINST. FIFTEEN DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND. ORDER(WITH ATTACHMENT) WAS SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: JOHN K. STEWART, REPORTER: PATRICK HEARD, CSR #11055

RULING:
ARGUED and the Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows --
DEFENDANT WILLIAM LUKENS, PHILLIP BASS, JENNIFER JONAK, DANIEL O'CONNELL'S Notice And Motion To Dismiss For Failure To Prosecute IS GRANTED WITH PREJUDICE. COUNSEL FOR JOINDER ASKED TO BE INCLUDED IN THE PROPOSED ORDER. THE COURT STATED THAT THE JOINDER MAY BE INCLUDED IN THE PROPOSED ORDER PROVIDED THAT THE JOINDER WAS PROPERLY FILED WITH THE COURT.
